key_insight: The paper learns a distribution-agnostic robust control correction on top of a nominal trajectory to satisfy chance constraints under sampled uncertainty.
engineering_relevance: It offers a reusable trajectory-optimization scaffold for spacecraft and landing guidance when uncertainty models are imperfect or non-Gaussian.
method: Compute an offline deterministic nominal trajectory.
Train reinforcement learning to add a structured affine closed-loop correction: feedforward adjustment plus time-varying feedback gains.
Enforce probabilistic feasibility with rollout-based upper-tail quantiles and terminal dispersion penalties.
evidence_signal: The framework was evaluated on a 3D Earth-Mars transfer and a stochastic rocket landing problem, including tests under unseen bounded and process disturbances.
watch_item: Performance depends on sampling quality and the empirical chance-constraint estimates may be sensitive to rollout count and scenario coverage.
paper_title: Distribution-Agnostic Robust Trajectory Optimization via Chance-Constrained Reinforcement Learning
